file_saver({"content": "Title: Scope and Counting Rules — Wikipedia Edits for “Antidisestablishmentarianism” up to June 2023

Objective
- Determine the total number of edits made to the English Wikipedia article “Antidisestablishmentarianism” from its inception through 2023-06-30 23:59:59 UTC.

Page Identity
- Project: English Wikipedia (enwiki)
- Title: Antidisestablishmentarianism
- Namespace: Article namespace (ns = 0)
- Scope limited strictly to this page’s history. Talk pages, user pages, and other namespaces are excluded.

Timeframe and Timezone
- Start: Page creation (earliest available revision for this page)
- End: Inclusive through 2023-06-30 23:59:59 UTC
- All timestamps interpreted in UTC (MediaWiki default)

What Counts as an “Edit”
- Count = number of revisions returned by the MediaWiki API for the page within the timeframe.
- Includes: minor edits, bot edits, revert edits, section edits, edits while the page was a redirect, edits associated with protection/unprotection that created a revision, and any other revision that appears in the public revision history.
- Revisions are identified uniquely by revid; counting uses the set of revisions with timestamps <= 2023-06-30 23:59:59 UTC.

What Is Excluded
- Deleted/suppressed revisions not visible to the public API (i.e., that cannot be retrieved without special privileges).
- Edits to other titles (including redirects pointing to this page under different titles) unless their histories were merged into this page and are now part of its revision list.
- Non-article namespaces (Talk, User, etc.).
- Log events (e.g., page moves, protections) that do not create an actual page revision.

Treatment of Page Moves, Redirects, and Merges
- Page moves: counted only insofar as they resulted in actual revisions on the page (moves themselves are log events and do not increment the revision count). The pageid remains consistent across standard moves; therefore, revisions tied to the current page history are in scope regardless of past titles.
- Redirect states: if the page was at any time a redirect, edits that created/edited the redirect are included as they are bona fide revisions.
- History merges/splits: if revisions from another title were merged into this page’s history (and are visible in the current revision list), they are included; revisions not present in the public revision list are excluded.

Null/Duplicate Edits
- Counting is strictly by distinct revids returned by the API. Any mediawiki behavior that does not create a new revid is not counted. If a “null edit” produced a new revision id, it is included like any other revision.

Primary Data Source and Cross-Check
- Primary: MediaWiki API (action=query&prop=revisions) for the page “Antidisestablishmentarianism,” retrieving all revisions with their timestamps and flags through the cutoff.
- Cross-check: XTools Page History (public) for the same page and timeframe. Any discrepancies will be investigated (e.g., due to deleted/suppressed revisions or history merges).

Counting Procedure (to be executed in the next step)
1) Use MediaWiki API to retrieve all revisions for the article, ordered oldest-to-newest, bounded by end timestamp 2023-06-30T23:59:59Z.
2) Count the number of unique revision IDs (revid) returned up to and including the cutoff timestamp.
3) Independently retrieve XTools summary for the same page; compare total edits through the cutoff.
4) If discrepancy exists, analyze potential causes (visibility of deleted revisions, namespace/title changes, history merges) and resolve to a final count based on publicly visible revisions.

Assumptions
- All counting uses UTC.
- Only publicly visible revisions are included (no admin-only deleted edits).
- The result is intended to be the public-history edit count for the article through June 2023 inclusive.
